attractions.name: A. Schwab (A. Schwab Dry Goods Store)
attractions.description: Established in 1876, A. Schwab is the oldest store and the only remaining original business on Beale Street. This historic dry goods store offers an eclectic collection of Memphis souvenirs, retro toys, and household items. It also features a functioning old-fashioned soda fountain and a museum dedicated to the rich history and culture of the region. Official Website, Memphis Travel
attractions.region: Downtown / Beale Street Historic District Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Sunday to Thursday: 11am-5pm; Friday to Saturday: 11am-7pm Official Website
attractions.days_open: Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Viator, Hoodoo Museum
highlights: 'Established in 1876, it is the oldest store and sole original business remaining on Beale Street. [Wikipedia', 'Authentic old-fashioned soda fountain offering handmade phosphate sodas, milkshakes, and gelato. Official Website', 'The Beale Street Museum and Hoodoo Museum featuring local historical artifacts and folklife exhibits. Hoodoo Museum', "Eclectic merchandise ranging from Memphis souvenirs and retro toys to 'hoodoo' powders and dry goods. Memphis Travel", 'Historic interior featuring a grand staircase and original mezzanine floors. Official Website']
attractions.tips: Try a handmade phosphate soda or a milkshake at the soda fountain to experience a classic 19th-century treat. If you are interested in local history, be sure to ask at the front desk about visiting the museum exhibits on the upper levels. Visit during the day for a more relaxed shopping environment before the Beale Street nightlife picks up. Tripadvisor, Choose901

attractions.name: Agricenter International
attractions.description: Agricenter International is a 1,000-acre non-profit organization dedicated to agricultural research, education, and conservation. As the world’s largest urban farm, it serves as a premier agritourism destination and a hands-on showcase for production agriculture, welcoming over 1.3 million visitors annually Agricenter International, Tennessee Home and Farm

attractions.name: Arcade Restaurant
attractions.description: The Arcade Restaurant is Memphis' oldest cafe, founded in 1919 by Greek immigrant Speros Zepatos. This historic diner features mid-century Greek Revival architecture, nostalgic boomerang table tops, and original neon signage that makes it a popular filming location for Hollywood movies. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.region: South Main Arts District / Downtown (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Saturday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Sunday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free entry to the restaurant; average meal price $10-$25 (Memphis Travel)
highlights: "Memphis' oldest cafe, operating since 1919 ([Official Website)", 'Famous for being a favorite hangout spot of Elvis Presley (Wikipedia)', "Featured in over 30 movies, including scenes in 'Mystery Train' and 'Walk the Line' (YouTube/Tennessee Crossroads)", 'Authentic 1950s diner atmosphere with original boomerang table tops (Official Website)', 'Serves breakfast all day alongside Southern-style daily lunch specials (Official Website)', 'Located in the historic South Main Arts District at the corner of G.E. Patterson (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Be sure to try the Sweet Potato Pancakes, a house specialty, and look for the booth where Elvis used to sit. The restaurant can be quite busy on weekends, so consider booking a table via Resy. If you visit on the last Friday of the month (Trolley Night), they may have extended evening hours. (Official Website, Memphis Travel)

attractions.name: AutoZone Park (Current home of Memphis Baseball)
attractions.description: AutoZone Park is a premiere Minor League Baseball stadium in downtown Memphis, serving as the home of the Triple-A Memphis Redbirds and Memphis 901 FC. Opened in 2000 to replace the historical Tim McCarver Stadium (where the Memphis Chicks played), it is celebrated for its neo-traditional brick architecture and views of the city skyline. MiLB.com, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Box Office (In-Season): Opens 90 minutes before game time and closes 90 minutes after first pitch. Non-Gameday Box Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM. Team Store: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. MiLB.com, Memphis Travel
attractions.days_open: Open daily during the baseball and soccer seasons (typically April–September). Administrative offices and the team store are open Monday through Friday year-round. MiLB.com
attractions.admission: Tickets range from approximately $5–$13 for general admission (The Bluffs) to $80 for all-inclusive club seats. Specialized "901 Tickets" are available for $9.01 in select sections for every home game. Children aged two and under are admitted free of charge but must sit on an adult's lap. MiLB.com, Stadium Journey, OurSports Central
highlights: ['Home field for the Triple-A Memphis Redbirds and Memphis 901 FC professional soccer team.', 'Features one of the largest HD video boards in the minor leagues, measuring 60 feet by 60 feet.', 'Unique "Bluffs" general admission lawn seating areas located in the outfield.', 'Open concourse design allowing fans to view the field from anywhere in the stadium.', 'Historical "Throwback Thursday" games where players wear Memphis Chicks jerseys to honor the city\'s baseball heritage.', "A rooftop garden that provides fresh ingredients for the stadium's specialty concessions."]
attractions.tips: AutoZone Park is a completely cashless venue, so ensure you have a credit card or mobile payment like Apple Pay ready for concessions and the team store. For the best value, bring a blanket for the grass-covered Bluffs or visit on 'Throwback Thursday' for discounted beers and hot dogs. Parking is most convenient in the attached Toyota garage, though cheaper street parking is often available nearby. MiLB.com, Stadium Journey
attractions.nearby: Beale Street Entertainment District, FedExForum, The Peabody Memphis (Peabody Ducks), National Civil Rights Museum, Memphis Rock 'n' Soul Museum. Memphis Travel, Downtown Memphis Commission

Opened in 2000 and built to Major League standards, the ballpark is celebrated for its intimate design, state-of-the-art amenities, and vibrant atmosphere that anchors the city's sports scene. Wikipedia, MLB.com
attractions.hours: Box Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM (non-gamedays); opens at 10:00 AM on gamedays. Gates typically open 1 hour before first pitch (5:00 PM on Fridays). Memphis Travel
attractions.days_open: Daily for scheduled events; Box Office open Monday–Saturday Memphis Travel
attractions.admission: Ticket prices range from as low as $6–$9.01 for Bluff/Lawn seating to approximately $55 for Redbirds Club access; children 2 years and under are free if sitting on an adult's lap Memphis Travel, OurSports Central
highlights: 'Home of the Triple-A Memphis Redbirds baseball team [MiLB.com', 'Voted as one of the top Minor League Ballparks in America Memphis Downtowner', 'Features a massive state-of-the-art LED video board MiLB.com', 'Offers an intimate spectator experience with approximately 10,000 capacity Wikipedia', 'Renowned as a top vegetarian-friendly ballpark with diverse concession options HoneyTrek']
attractions.tips: AutoZone Park is a completely cashless venue and enforces a strict clear bag policy. Arrive at least one hour early to catch batting practice, and consider Bluff seating for a budget-friendly, relaxed experience on the grass. MiLB.com, Facebook

attractions.name: Bass Pro Shops at the Pyramid
attractions.description: Bass Pro Shops at the Pyramid is a massive wilderness-themed destination located inside the iconic 321-foot-tall Memphis Pyramid. This 535,000-square-foot facility features a cypress swamp with live alligators, the world's tallest freestanding elevator, and a luxury hotel called Big Cypress Lodge. (Bass Pro Shops, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Store: Monday-Saturday 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sunday 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Observation Deck: Monday-Saturday 11:00 AM - 8:30 PM, Sunday 11:00 AM - 6:30 PM (Bass Pro Shops, Big Cypress Lodge)
attractions.admission: Store Entry: Free; Sky High Ride & Observation Deck: Adults $8.00, Children (4-12) $5.00, Big Cypress Lodge Guests: Free (Big Cypress Lodge)
highlights: "28-story world's tallest freestanding elevator with glass-floored observation decks ([Big Cypress Lodge)", 'The Fishbowl: 13 lanes of underwater-themed bowling with ocean-themed decor (Bass Pro Shops)', 'Ducks Unlimited Waterfowling Heritage Center, a museum dedicated to wetlands conservation (Bass Pro Shops)', 'Big Cypress Lodge: A 103-room rustic-elegant hotel located entirely within the Pyramid (Big Cypress Lodge)', '600,000-gallon aquarium and cypress swamp environment featuring live alligators and fish (Bass Pro Shops)', 'Wahlburgers Wild, offering a unique wilderness-inspired menu (Big Cypress Lodge)']
attractions.tips: Visitors recommend planning for at least half a day to explore the entire facility. The observation deck offers spectacular views of the Mississippi River and Memphis skyline, especially at sunset, but it can be quite windy. Daily live fish feedings take place at 2:00 PM. (Tripadvisor, Bass Pro Shops)

attractions.name: Bass Pro Shops at the Pyramid (Bass Pro Shops)
attractions.description: The Memphis Pyramid is a massive 535,000-square-foot destination that has been transformed into a wilderness-inspired experience. It features an indoor cypress swamp with 100-foot tall trees, live fish, and alligators, along with the luxury Big Cypress Lodge hotel. Visitors can enjoy unique dining, a bowling alley, and panoramic views from the world's tallest freestanding elevator and its glass-floored observation deck (Memphis Travel, Bass Pro Shops).

attractions.admission: Free admission to the store and swamp; Observation Deck: $8 for adults, $5 for children (ages 5-12); Bowling: $32/hour (Mon-Thu), $37/hour (Fri-Sun) plus $4.25 shoe rental (Bass Pro Shops, Big Cypress Lodge)
highlights: "World's tallest freestanding elevator rising 28 stories to a glass observation deck ([Bass Pro Shops)", 'Indoor cypress swamp featuring 100-foot trees, live alligators, and large aquariums (Memphis Travel)', 'The Fishbowl at the Pyramid, an underwater-themed bowling alley with 13 lanes (Big Cypress Lodge)', 'Ducks Unlimited Waterfowling Heritage Center, a museum dedicated to conservation and history (Ducks Unlimited)', 'The Lookout at the Pyramid, a restaurant and bar offering spectacular views of Memphis (Bass Pro Shops)', 'Big Cypress Lodge, a wilderness-themed luxury hotel built directly inside the pyramid (Big Cypress Lodge)']
attractions.tips: Plan for at least half a day to explore the various attractions inside. Be sure to catch the live fish feeding show daily at 2:00 PM. If you're interested in bowling, arrive early as it is first-come, first-served. Guests staying at the Big Cypress Lodge receive complimentary elevator access to the observation deck (Yelp, Bass Pro Shops).
attractions.nearby: Mud Island River Park, Renasant Convention Center, Sun Studio, National Civil Rights Museum, Beale Street (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Beale Street Entertainment District (Beale Street)
attractions.description: Beale Street is a historic three-block entertainment district in downtown Memphis, Tennessee, famously known as the 'Home of the Blues' and the birthplace of Rock 'n' Roll. It is lined with iconic blues clubs, restaurants, and retail shops that offer live music every night of the year, showcasing a melting pot of delta blues, jazz, and R&B (Beale Street, Memphis Travel).
attractions.address: 203 Beale Street, Suite 300 (Management), Beale St (District), Memphis, TN 38103 (Beale Street Management)
attractions.hours: District is accessible 24/7; individual businesses typically open from 11:00 am until late night (up to 3:00 am or 5:00 am). Security fees and age restrictions (21+) often begin at 9:00 pm on busy nights (Beale Street FAQ, Tripadvisor).
attractions.days_open: Open 365 days a year (Beale Street Calendar)
attractions.admission: Free entry during the day; $5 Beale Street Security Fee may apply after 9:00 pm on select Friday, Saturday, and holiday nights (Beale Street FAQ). Some venues may charge an additional cover fee or offer a $10 all-access pass (Tripadvisor).
highlights: "Historic blues clubs and live music venues like B.B. King's Blues Club and Rum Boogie Cafe ([Beale Street)", 'Handy Park, an outdoor performing arts space dedicated to W.C. Handy (Beale Street)', 'The Beale Street Brass Note Walk of Fame, honoring over 100 musical legends (Carpe Travel)', 'A. Schwab Dry Goods Store, the oldest store on Beale Street, operating since 1876 (Expedia)', 'The W.C. Handy House Museum and the Withers Collection Museum & Gallery (Beale Street)']
attractions.tips: Visit during the day for a more family-friendly atmosphere; after 9:00 pm, the street often becomes 21+ and a security fee may apply. Wear comfortable walking shoes and be sure to check out the 'flippers'—street performers who do acrobatics down the center of the street (Beale Street FAQ, Carpe Travel).
attractions.nearby: FedExForum, AutoZone Park, The Peabody Memphis, National Civil Rights Museum, Orpheum Theatre, Sun Studio (Beale Street Nearby Attractions)

attractions.name: Big River Crossing
attractions.description: Big River Crossing is the longest public pedestrian and cyclist bridge across the Mississippi River, stretching nearly one mile. It connects the downtown areas of Memphis, Tennessee, and West Memphis, Arkansas, serving as a key link for recreation and transit. Visitors can enjoy panoramic views of the river and skyline, which are further enhanced by the bridge's nightly Mighty Lights LED display. (Big River Crossing, Memphis Travel)
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM Daily (Big River Crossing)
attractions.admission: Free (Big River Crossing)
highlights: 'Longest public pedestrian and cyclist bridge across the Mississippi River ([Big River Crossing)', "Features the 'Mighty Lights' nightly LED display (Memphis Travel)", 'Connects Main Street in Memphis to Main Street in West Memphis (We Are Memphis)', 'Provides access to the 1,500-acre Ducks Unlimited Park in Arkansas (Arkansas.com)', 'Offers breathtaking views of the Memphis skyline and Arkansas floodplains (Big River Crossing)']
attractions.tips: Visit at sunset to experience the transition to the 'Mighty Lights' display. Cyclists should yield to pedestrians and alert them before passing on the left. Free parking is available on both the Tennessee and Arkansas sides. (Memphis Travel)
attractions.nearby: Martyrs Park, Metal Museum, Tom Lee Park, National Civil Rights Museum, Beale Street, Ducks Unlimited Park (Downtown Memphis Commission)

attractions.name: Binghampton (Memphis, TN)
attractions.description: Binghampton is a historic and diverse neighborhood located in the geographic center of Memphis, Tennessee, representing a blend of residential, commercial, and industrial heritage. Originally a racially integrated town in the late 1800s, it is now celebrated for its vibrant Broad Avenue Arts District, which features public murals, locally owned boutiques, and a burgeoning culinary scene. (Binghampton Development Corporation, Homes.com)
attractions.address: 280 Tillman St (Binghampton Development Corporation), Broad Avenue Arts District (Broad Ave & Sam Cooper Blvd)
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours in the Arts District typically vary from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM) (Broad Avenue Arts District)
attractions.admission: Free (Homes.com, Broad Avenue Arts District)
highlights: ['Broad Avenue Arts District with 70+ local businesses, galleries, and boutiques', 'Signature neighborhood water tower and numerous public art murals', 'The Hampline, a major bicycle and pedestrian trail connecting to Overton Park', 'Binghampton Park, a local community green space', 'Historic 1920s bungalows and revitalized industrial architecture', 'Active community revitalization led by the Binghampton Development Corporation (BDC)']
attractions.tips: Visit on 'First Fridays' for special events and extended gallery hours in the Broad Avenue Arts District. The neighborhood is best explored by bike via the Hampline, and visitors should look for the iconic painted water tower as a central landmark. (Broad Avenue Arts District, Choose901)
attractions.nearby: Overton Park (Memphis Zoo, Memphis Brooks Museum of Art), Shelby Farms Greenline, Rhodes College, Crosstown Concourse (Memphis Travel)

attractions.name: Cannon Center for the Performing Arts
attractions.description: The Cannon Center for the Performing Arts is a 2,051-seat multi-purpose venue located in the heart of Downtown Memphis. Home to the Memphis Symphony Orchestra, the center features state-of-the-art acoustics and hosts a diverse range of events including concerts, ballet, opera, and touring Broadway productions (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM; also opens two hours prior to all ticketed events (Official Website).
attractions.admission: Varies by performance; historical averages range from $113 to $123 (Vivid Seats), with student discounts sometimes available for as low as $5 (Tripadvisor).
highlights: 'Home to the Memphis Symphony Orchestra ([Wikipedia)', 'State-of-the-art acoustics and lighting (Official Website)', 'Classic horseshoe seating configuration with unobstructed views (Memphis Travel)', 'Adjacent to the newly modernized Renasant Convention Center (Renasant Convention Center)', 'Historical significance as the site of the former Ellis Auditorium (CityInk)']
attractions.tips: The venue is cashless; ensure you have a credit or debit card for all purchases (Official Website). It is recommended to reserve parking in advance at the Renasant/Cannon garage located at 266 N. Front St. (Official Website). Arrive at least 90 minutes early for Memphis Symphony Orchestra events to enjoy pre-concert talks (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Renasant Convention Center, Bass Pro Shops at the Pyramid, Mud Island River Park, Beale Street, and the National Civil Rights Museum (Expedia, Downtown Memphis Commission).

attractions.name: Central BBQ (Downtown Location)
attractions.description: Central BBQ is a renowned barbecue joint in Memphis celebrated for its authentic Southern flavors, competition-style meats, and inviting atmosphere. Since opening in 2002, it has consistently been ranked as a top spot for BBQ, known for its slow-smoked meats using hickory and pecan woods, along with its signature dry rubs and diverse sauces.
attractions.address: 147 E Butler Ave, Memphis, TN 38103, USA
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Prices for food vary: $6.50 - $35.00) Central BBQ Menu
highlights: ['Award-winning Memphis-style ribs (wet, dry, or half & half)', 'Famous Pulled Pork BBQ Nachos topped with cheese sauce, jalapeños, and BBQ Shake', 'Slow-smoked beef brisket, turkey breast, and meaty wings', 'Homemade peanut butter pie and banana pudding for dessert', 'Competition-style cooking perfected since the early 1980s by founders Craig Blondis and Roger Sapp']
attractions.tips: The line often moves quickly despite being a popular spot, and there is ample seating available. It is located directly behind the National Civil Rights Museum, making it a perfect lunch stop after a visit. Street parking is generally not an issue nearby. Yelp, YouTube

attractions.name: Charlie Vergos' Rendezvous
attractions.description: Charlie Vergos' Rendezvous is a legendary Memphis barbecue institution founded in 1948, famous for its signature charcoal-grilled dry-rub ribs. Located in a historic basement accessible via a downtown alley, it offers a unique atmosphere filled with antiques and memorabilia that captures the quintessential spirit of Memphis. Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Friday & Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Sunday & Monday: Closed Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (No cover charge; pay for food and drinks) Airial Travel
highlights: 'Signature dry-rub ribs cooked over charcoal in a historic coal chute vent [Official Website', 'Authentic basement dining experience with vintage Memphis decor Tripadvisor', 'Family-owned and operated for over four generations Official Website', 'Famous cheese and sausage plates featuring smoked sausage and cheddar SinglePlatform', 'Celebrity hotspot visited by presidents and music icons Airial Travel']
attractions.tips: Look for the entrance in the alley across from the Peabody Hotel; GPS may seem to lead you to an empty alley, but that’s the correct spot. Be prepared for a wait during peak hours as it operates on a first-come, first-served basis. Valet parking is available at the corner of Monroe and the Rendezvous Alley. Airial Travel, Official Website

attractions.name: Chickasaw Gardens (Chickasaw Gardens Park)
attractions.description: Chickasaw Gardens is a historic and upscale neighborhood in midtown Memphis known for its lush, park-like environment and grand residential architecture. The community centers around a 22-acre lake featuring a scenic fountain and a popular walking trail, all situated on land that was formerly part of the estate of Clarence Saunders, founder of Piggly Wiggly. (Chickasaw Gardens HOA, Wikipedia)

attractions.tips: Fishing and swimming in the lake are strictly prohibited. Visitors should observe the neighborhood's 15 mph speed limit and are encouraged to visit during spring or fall when the flora is in full bloom. (Chickasaw Gardens HOA, Pacer Walking App)

attractions.description: The Children's Museum of Memphis creates memorable learning experiences through the joy of play in hands-on exhibits and programs. Housed in a former National Guard Armory, the museum features over 30 interactive exhibits, including a restored 1909 carousel and a large seasonal splash park. It is a premier destination for families in Midtown Memphis, aimed primarily at children between the ages of 2 and 10. (Children's Museum of Memphis, Memphis Travel)

attractions.description: Cooper-Young is an eclectic and vibrant historic district in Midtown Memphis, known for its creative spirit and unique personality. It features a diverse mix of century-old homes, family-owned businesses, independent shops, and award-winning restaurants, and was recognized as one of the "10 Great Neighborhoods in America" by the American Planning Association.

attractions.description: Crosstown Brewing Company is a craft brewery and taproom located in the historic Crosstown Concourse area of Memphis. Founded by Will Goodwin and Clark Ortkiese, it focuses on building community through high-quality beer and a welcoming social environment. (Official Website)

It is a modest Baptist church that has become a world-renowned destination for its powerful, high-energy Southern gospel services and the charismatic presence of Bishop Green himself. While it serves as a local house of worship, it welcomingly hosts a diverse international congregation drawn to its spiritual message and legendary musical heritage.

Originally opened as a movie theatre in 1937, it was converted into a premier concert hall in the 1980s and has since hosted a wide array of local and national touring acts. Following a brief closure, the venue reopened in 2023, continuing its legacy as a staple of the Memphis music scene Beale Street, Wikipedia.

attractions.description: The Cotton Museum at the Memphis Cotton Exchange is a historical and cultural institution located on the restored former trading floor of the Memphis Cotton Exchange. It tells the story of 'King Cotton' and its profound impact on the culture, economy, and history of Memphis, the South, and the global economy. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 65 Union Ave, Memphis, TN 38103 Official Website
attractions.region: Downtown / Cotton Row Historic District Tripadvisor, Expedia
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: Closed Official Website
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday Official Website
attractions.admission: $10 Adults; $9 Students, Teachers, Seniors; $8 Military and First Responders; $8 Children 6-12; Free for children under 6 Official Website
highlights: 'Restored historic Trade Floor of the Memphis Cotton Exchange [Official Website', 'Original architectural features and 1920s-1970s artifacts Tennessee Home and Farm', 'Large trade board with price quotes from 1939 Tennessee Home and Farm', 'Interactive oral histories accessible in former phone booths TN History For Kids', "'The Story of Cotton' mural by artist David Mah Tennessee Home and Farm", 'Self-guided audio tour of the Cotton Row Historic District Expedia']
attractions.tips: Plan for at least 1.5 to 2 hours to fully explore the detailed exhibits and oral histories Wonderful Museums. Arrive early as last admission is at 4 PM, one hour before closing Official Website. Street parking and the adjacent 'Parking Can Be Fun' garage are available nearby Memphis Travel.

attractions.name: The Four Way Restaurant
attractions.description: The Four Way Restaurant is a historic soul food landmark in Memphis' Soulsville USA neighborhood, originally opened in 1946 as the Four Way Grill. It served as a vital meeting place and safe haven during the Civil Rights Movement, hosting leaders like Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and legendary musicians from nearby Stax Records. Today, it continues to serve generational Southern recipes in a dining room filled with photographs and memorabilia chronicling its storied history.
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Jazz Brunch 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM); Sunday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Closed every 5th Sunday)
attractions.admission: Free (Menu prices vary; e.g., Fried Green Tomatoes $4.89 - $6.89, Party Wings $7.99)
highlights: ['Historic Civil Rights landmark where Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. frequently dined', 'Generational soul food recipes including fried catfish, turkey and dressing, and peach cobbler', 'Former "commissary" for Stax Records artists like Otis Redding and Isaac Hayes', 'Features a significant community mural designed by Memphis artist Danielle Sierra', 'Family-owned and operated by the Bates family since 2002', 'Located in the heart of the historic Soulsville USA neighborhood']
attractions.tips: Visitors often recommend the fried catfish and peach cobbler, which were favorites of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. The restaurant can be busy during peak times, especially after church on Sundays, so be prepared for a potential wait. Orders are typically placed at the front counter before you are seated. Don't miss the chance to view the extensive collection of historical photos and the outdoor mural.
attractions.nearby: Stax Museum of American Soul Music, LeMoyne-Owen College, Elmwood Cemetery, National Civil Rights Museum

attractions.name: The Peabody Memphis
attractions.description: The Peabody Memphis is a world-renowned historic luxury hotel in downtown Memphis, affectionately known as the "South's Grand Hotel." Opened in 1925, the Italian Renaissance-style landmark is famous for its daily Duck March tradition and its role as a cultural hub for the Mississippi Delta. It is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and remains one of the most iconic destinations in the American South The Peabody Memphis, Wikipedia.
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours (Duck March: Daily 11:00 AM and 5:00 PM) The Peabody Memphis, Yelp
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week) The Peabody Memphis
attractions.admission: Free to enter the lobby and watch the Duck March; Hotel History Tours: $15 per person (children 12 and under are free) The Peabody Memphis, Memphis Travel
highlights: 'Daily Peabody Duck March at 11 a.m. and 5 p.m. [The Peabody Memphis', "Rooftop 'Duck Palace' and panoramic city views Forbes Travel Guide", 'Elegant Italian Renaissance lobby with a 1925 travertine marble fountain Historic Hotels of America', 'Lansky Bros. clothing boutique, famously the clothier for Elvis Presley Postcards from Abroad', 'Traditional English Afternoon Tea at the Forbes Four-Star Chez Philippe Forbes Travel Guide', 'Memorabilia Room on the mezzanine level featuring historic artifacts and Elvis Presley’s RCA contract Postcards from Abroad']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 to 60 minutes early to secure a good spot for the Duck March, especially if you want to be near the red carpet. For a bird's-eye view, head to the mezzanine level. Don't miss the rooftop Duck Palace and the sunset happy hours during the summer Memphis Travel, Tripadvisor.
attractions.nearby: Beale Street Entertainment District, Memphis Rock 'n' Soul Museum, National Civil Rights Museum, Orpheum Theatre, FedExForum, Sun Studio Tripadvisor, Forbes Travel Guide

attractions.name: The University of Memphis (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The University of Memphis is a major public research institution located in the heart of Memphis, Tennessee. Founded in 1912, it serves over 20,000 students and is recognized for its "R1" status for very high research activity across more than 250 areas of study (Wikipedia). The campus serves as a cultural hub, featuring the Art Museum of the University of Memphis and a vibrant student life (University of Memphis).
attractions.address: 3720 Alumni Ave, Memphis, TN 38152 (University of Memphis)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Administrative Hours) (University of Memphis)
attractions.admission: Free (University of Memphis, AAA)
highlights: 'Classified as an R1 research university with top-tier academic programs ([Wikipedia).', 'Home to the Art Museum of the University of Memphis (AMUM) featuring ancient Egyptian artifacts (Wikipedia).', 'Rich athletic tradition as the home of the Memphis Tigers (Wikipedia).', 'Dynamic campus life with over 200 student organizations (Wikipedia).', 'Historic architecture including the iconic Administration Building and Alumni Mall (Wikipedia).']
attractions.tips: Official campus tours must be scheduled 72 hours in advance and are limited to one student and two guests (University of Memphis). If visiting the Art Museum on a Saturday, parking in the Central lot is free (University of Memphis). A self-guided tour script is available for those who prefer to explore at their own pace (University of Memphis).
attractions.nearby: Highland Strip (Dining & Nightlife) (St. Jude Guide), Pink Palace Museum (MoSH) (Tripadvisor), Memphis Botanic Garden (AAA), Liberty Stadium (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Theatre Memphis
attractions.description: Theatre Memphis is a non-profit community theatre that has entertained patrons for over 100 years. Its mission is to provide outstanding theatrical experiences to enrich the lives of its diverse audiences, participants, and community through professional-quality productions and educational outreach Theatre Memphis About, Yelp.
attractions.address: 630 Perkins Extd., Memphis, TN 38117 Theatre Memphis

attractions.name: Tom Lee Park
attractions.description: Tom Lee Park is a vibrant 31-acre riverfront oasis on the Mississippi River in downtown Memphis, recently revitalized through a $61 million transformation. The park serves as a national model for inclusive urban spaces and ecological restoration, offering a blend of active recreation areas and tranquil natural landscapes. It is named in honor of Tom Lee, a local hero who saved 32 people from a sinking steamboat in 1925.
attractions.hours: Park: 5 am - 10 pm (Mar-Oct), 5 am - 8 pm (Nov-Feb); Welcome Center and Courts: 8 am - 8 pm (Mar-Oct), 9 am - 7 pm (Nov-Feb); Hi Neighbor Café: Mon-Fri 4 pm - 7:30 pm & Sat-Sun 11:30 am - 7:30 pm (Mar-Oct) Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Casago, Memphis Parks
highlights: ["Life on the River Playground: A 23,000-square-foot playscape inspired by the Mississippi River's hydrology.", 'Sunset Canopy: A 15,000-square-foot multi-use pavilion featuring basketball and pickleball courts.', "A Monument to Listening: An evocative public art installation by artist Theaster Gates honoring Tom Lee's legacy.", 'Hammock Grove: A peaceful area equipped with hammocks for relaxing with views of the river.', 'Carlisle Cutbank Bluff: A newly designed ADA-accessible entrance that seamlessly connects the park to the city bluff.', 'Hi Neighbor Café: A local eatery offering refreshments, including seasonal food and drinks with riverfront views.']
attractions.tips: The park is best accessed by walking from downtown Memphis, with over 2,000 parking spots located within a few blocks. Visitors can borrow basketball and pickleball equipment for free at the Sunset Canopy. For the best experience, visit during sunset to enjoy panoramic views of the river and the Memphis-Arkansas Bridge lights. Official Website, Memphis Travel
attractions.nearby: Beale Street, National Civil Rights Museum, Blues Hall of Fame Museum, Orpheum Theatre, Big Cypress Lodge at the Pyramid Memphis Travel, Casago

attractions.name: Tops Bar-B-Q
attractions.description: Tops Bar-B-Q is a legendary Memphis institution that has been serving authentic, hickory-smoked barbecue and fresh-ground burgers since 1952. With 17 locations across the Mid-South, it is a beloved local staple known for its slow-cooked pork shoulder, St. Louis style ribs, and time-honored family recipes (Tops Bar-B-Q About).
attractions.hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM; Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM; Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM; Thursday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM; Friday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM; Saturday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM; Sunday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM (Tops Bar-B-Q Union Ave)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant) (Tops Bar-B-Q Menu)
highlights: 'Hickory-smoked pork shoulder cooked on-site for hours ([Tops Bar-B-Q)', 'Famous fresh-ground burgers cooked to order (Tops Bar-B-Q)', 'St. Louis style ribs seasoned and slow-smoked with hardwood charcoal (Tops Bar-B-Q Menu)', 'Memphis-style sides including Bar-B-Q beans, coleslaw, and banana pudding (Tops Bar-B-Q Menu)', 'Breakfast served daily until 10:30 AM with unique items like the Brisket, Egg & Cheese sandwich (Tops Bar-B-Q Menu)', 'Historic local chain with over 70 years of service in the Memphis community (Tops Bar-B-Q About)']
attractions.tips: Breakfast is served daily but only until 10:30 AM. For a true local experience, try the 'Fireman' or 'Policeman' burgers which combine their famous burger patties with BBQ pork or brisket. The Union Ave location is a convenient stop when visiting nearby attractions like Sun Studio (Tops Bar-B-Q Menu, Downtown Memphis Commission).
attractions.nearby: Sun Studio, Woodruff-Fontaine House Museum, Memphis Zoo, Memphis Botanic Garden (Downtown Memphis Commission, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Victorian Village Historic District
attractions.description: Victorian Village is a historic district in Memphis, Tennessee, once known as 'Millionaire's Row' due to the concentration of grand 19th-century mansions. The neighborhood features beautifully preserved architecture ranging from Neo-classical to Late Gothic Revival, with several homes now serving as public museums and luxury bed-and-breakfasts.
attractions.hours: Woodruff-Fontaine: Wed-Sun 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM; Mallory-Neely: Fri-Sat tours at 10:30 AM, 11:30 AM, 1:30 PM, 2:30 PM, 3:30 PM; Magevney House: 1st Saturday of month 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM.
attractions.days_open: Wednesday to Sunday (specific to Woodruff-Fontaine); Friday and Saturday (Mallory-Neely); First Saturday of the month (Magevney).
attractions.admission: Woodruff-Fontaine House: Adults $16, Seniors (65+) $13, Children (6-12) $11; Mallory-Neely House: Non-members $16, Members $8; Magevney House: Free.
highlights: ['Woodruff-Fontaine House Museum, a grand French Victorian mansion from 1870.', 'Mallory-Neely House, one of the few historic properties to retain almost all original furnishings.', 'The Magevney House, one of the oldest residences in Memphis and site of the first Catholic Mass.', "The James Lee House, a meticulously restored riverboat tycoon's mansion now operating as a luxury B&B.", 'Mollie Fontaine Lounge, a trendy cocktail lounge housed in a historic Victorian mansion.']
attractions.tips: Visitors should take their time to explore on foot to appreciate the intricate architectural details. It is recommended to check the specific tour schedules for each house in advance, as they have limited public hours. The area is best visited during the day, and spring or fall offer the best weather for viewing the gardens.
attractions.nearby: Sun Studio, Edge Motor Museum, Health Sciences Park, St. Mary's Episcopal Cathedral, AutoZone Park.

attractions.name: W.C. Handy Memphis Home and Museum
attractions.description: The W.C. Handy Memphis Home and Museum is a historic two-room shotgun house that once belonged to William Christopher Handy, the "Father of the Blues." Originally located on Jeanette Place, the modest clapboard shack was moved to Beale Street in the mid-1980s and restored to serve as an interpretive center for the musician's life and professional achievements (Beale Street, Frommer's).
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sunday - Monday: Closed (Tripadvisor, Frommer's)
attractions.admission: Adults: $6.00, Youth (4-17): $4.00, Children (3 and under): Free (Frommer's, Official Site)
highlights: 'The original desk where W.C. Handy wrote many of his most famous songs, including "The Memphis Blues" and "St. Louis Blues" ([Beale Street).', 'A historic two-room "shotgun" house architecture, typical of the early 20th-century Mid-South (Go Eat Do).', "Extensive collection of personal artifacts, memorabilia, and historical photographs reflecting the society and times of Handy's era (Official Site).", 'Guided tours that provide informative commentary on the "Father of the Blues" and the history of Memphis music (Frommer\'s).']
attractions.tips: Visitors are strongly encouraged to call ahead to confirm hours of operation, as the museum is often closed unexpectedly due to staffing (Airial Travel). Photography is strictly prohibited inside the museum (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Handy Park, Withers Collection Museum & Gallery, FedExForum, AutoZone Park, The New Daisy Theatre (Beale Street).

attractions.name: W.C. Handy Performing Arts Park
attractions.description: W.C. Handy Performing Arts Park is a vibrant urban greenspace located in the heart of the historic Beale Street Entertainment District. Named after the "Father of the Blues," the park features a iconic bronze statue of W.C. Handy and an open-air amphitheater that regularly hosts live blues performances and community festivals. It serves as a popular gathering spot for both locals and visitors to enjoy the musical heritage of Memphis in a relaxed, outdoor setting. (Memphis Parks, Landscape Architect)
attractions.hours: Daily 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM (Summer); 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Winter) (Memphis Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Most scheduled events and concerts are free to the public; some exclusive events may require a ticket) (AAA Trip Canvas, Beale Street)
highlights: ['Iconic bronze statue of W.C. Handy, the "Father of the Blues"', 'Open-air amphitheater for live concerts and community events', 'Historic markers detailing the rich musical history of Beale Street', 'Passive urban greenspace ideal for relaxation and people-watching', 'Regular venue for free live blues music and street performers']
attractions.tips: Visit in the evening to experience live music from local buskers and street performers. For scheduled concerts, consider bringing a lawn chair or blanket for seating on the grass. The park is located within the Beale Street open-container area, allowing visitors to enjoy drinks while watching performances. (AAA Trip Canvas, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: FedExForum, W.C. Handy Home and Museum, Peabody Hotel (Peabody Ducks), AutoZone Park, I AM A MAN Plaza (Beale Street, Memphis Travel)

attractions.name: Whitehaven
attractions.description: Whitehaven is a historic and culturally dynamic neighborhood in South Memphis, Tennessee, most famous as the location of Elvis Presley’s Graceland estate. Originally developed as a residential suburb in the 1950s and 1960s, it was annexed by the City of Memphis in 1970 and has since evolved into a predominantly African-American community and a major regional hub for tourism and shopping.
attractions.address: 3205 Elvis Presley Blvd, Memphis, TN 38116 (Memphis Visitor Center)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Neighborhood); Memphis Visitor Center: Wednesday–Saturday, 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Graceland: The iconic mansion and museum of Elvis Presley, attracting thousands of visitors annually.', 'Southland Mall: The first enclosed mall in the Memphis region, serving as a primary shopping destination.', 'David Carnes Park: A recently renovated 5-acre park featuring a splash pad, walking trails, and fitness equipment.', 'Whitehaven High School: A cornerstone institution known for its strong community spirit and academic magnet programs.', "Elvis Presley Boulevard: The neighborhood's main artery, famous for its music-themed decor and local soul food eateries."]
attractions.tips: Visit during "Elvis Week" in August to experience the community's most vibrant celebrations, and be sure to stop by local favorites like Marlowe’s Ribs or Pollard’s BBQ for authentic Memphis soul food. Use the Memphis Visitor Center on Elvis Presley Blvd for detailed maps and tourist information.
attractions.nearby: Graceland, Stax Museum of American Soul Music, Full Gospel Tabernacle Church, Memphis International Airport, T.O. Fuller State Park

attractions.name: Wiseacre Brewing Company
attractions.description: Wiseacre Brewing Company was founded by brothers Davin and Kellan Bartosch to create top-quality craft beer in their hometown of Memphis. They are renowned for their award-winning lagers, including Tiny Bomb and Sky Dog, and operate two distinct taprooms that serve as community hubs for beer lovers. (Wiseacre Brewing Co)

attractions.nearby: Broad Avenue Arts District, Memphis Zoo, Sun Studio, Museum of Science & History (MoSH), Overton Park, and the National Civil Rights Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Meeman-Shelby Forest State Park (Tennessee State Parks)
attractions.description: Meeman-Shelby Forest State Park is a sprawling 12,539-acre natural oasis located just north of Memphis along the Mississippi River. The park features a diverse landscape of mature bottomland hardwood forests, towering bluffs, and two serene lakes, Poplar Tree Lake and Lake Piersol. It is a haven for outdoor enthusiasts, offering extensive hiking trails, a championship disc golf course, and abundant wildlife viewing opportunities (Tennessee State Parks, Memphis Travel).
attractions.address: 910 Riddick Road, Millington, TN 38053 (Tennessee State Parks)
attractions.hours: Park: Monday-Sunday 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Visitor Center: Monday-Friday 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Saturday-Sunday 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Tennessee State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free for park entry; fees apply for camping, cabins, and boat rentals (Memphis Travel, Tennessee State Parks)
highlights: 'Over 20 miles of hiking trails, including the popular 3-mile Woodland Trail ([Tennessee State Parks)', 'A world-class 36-hole wooded disc golf course divided into two 18-hole segments (Memphis Travel)', 'Boating and fishing opportunities on the 125-acre Poplar Tree Lake (Tennessee State Parks)', 'The Meeman Museum and Nature Center, featuring local wildlife exhibits and an outdoor aviary (HOPS AND SKIPS)', 'Diverse wildlife including American Bald Eagles, deer, bobcats, and over 200 bird species (Memphis Travel)', 'Six rustic vacation cabins and 49 campsites with full hookups (Tennessee State Parks)']
attractions.tips: Be sure to visit the historic Shelby Forest General Store just outside the park for their famous burgers and live music nights. Bring bug spray if you plan to hike near the river or low-lying swamp areas, and check the Nature Center's seasonal hours before visiting (Reddit, HOPS AND SKIPS).
attractions.nearby: Mississippi River, Shelby Forest General Store, Millington, Memphis International Raceway, and downtown Memphis (approx. 15-20 minutes away) (Reddit, Expedia)

attractions.name: Big Hill Pond State Park Official Website
attractions.description: Big Hill Pond State Park encompasses over 4,000 acres of timberland and hardwood bottomland in southwestern Tennessee. The park is characterized by its unique wetland ecosystems, featuring the namesake 35-acre Big Hill Pond and the picturesque Travis McNatt Lake. Visitors can explore more than 30 miles of hiking trails and see historic Civil War earthworks. Official Website, Wikipedia

attractions.admission: Free entry to the park; fees apply for camping ($15/night) and pavilion rentals ($60/day). Official Website, Pick Tennessee Products
highlights: '70-foot Observation Tower with panoramic views [Official Website', 'Half-mile Dismal Swamp Boardwalk Official Website', 'Over 30 miles of hiking and 14 miles of equestrian trails Official Website', '35-acre Big Hill Pond with cypress stands Official Website', 'Travis McNatt Lake for fishing and paddling Official Website', 'Civil War earthworks and historical fortifications Official Website']
attractions.tips: Cell service is limited south of the railroad tracks; visit the observation tower at sunset for stunning views. Bring your own horses for equestrian trails as rentals are not available on-site. Official Website

attractions.description: The Holly Springs National Forest is a 155,000-acre natural refuge in north-central Mississippi, established in 1936 to restore depleted agricultural lands. It features a mosaic of pine and hardwood forests, sparkling lakes, and diverse wildlife, providing a serene escape for outdoor enthusiasts.

highlights: ['Centered around the 60-acre, spring-fed Spring Lake for fishing and scenic views.', 'Two 18-hole disc golf courses: Spring Creek and Turkey Hollow.', 'A 2.5-mile nature trail that winds around the lake through wooded areas.', 'Extensive lodging options including 64 developed campsites, 17 primitive campsites, and 9 air-conditioned cabins.', 'Visitor Center and meeting rooms that can accommodate large groups up to 200 people.', 'Historical significance as one of the original state parks built by the Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC).']
attractions.tips: Bring bug spray, especially for the wooded disc golf courses and trails. Reservations are strongly recommended for cabins and developed campsites, especially during Ole Miss football weekends or October events. Note that while boating is allowed, swimming in the lake is no longer permitted. Cruise America, StateParks.com Reviews
